summary: P0 Scalability regression + new-feature verification. All 74 existing + 4 new contract tests pass (78/78). Pagination backward-compat confirmed on /talents, /projects, /links, /applications, /submissions/approved, and /projects/{pid}/submissions (raw list when ?page absent, envelope {items,total,page,size,has_more} when present). 150 MB video cap + 25 MB image cap enforced on both public submission and public application uploads with matching 400 'too large' error. Image resize pipeline generates 1600px JPEG resized_storage_path on submissions AND applications image uploads. SubmissionPage.jsx progress-bar code verified via static review: axios onUploadProgress → setUploadPct → UploadSlot renders Uploading… N% label + filling stripe; AddImage button shows % + stripe; the single shared uploadPct prop flows to UploadSlot and AddTakeSlot. Public submit route is /submit/{slug} (App.js L72) — the review-request reference to /audition/{slug} was stale.

issues: Uses native browser mm/dd/yyyy input instead of the shadcn Calendar/Popover pattern used elsewhere. Inconsistent with brand dark theme; no visible date-format hint near the field.

action_items: Optional: consider swapping the native <input type=date> for the shadcn Calendar used elsewhere in the app for design consistency.
Stale context in task brief: public audition URL is /submit/{slug}, not /audition/{slug}. Update the problem statement/docs.
critical_code_review_comments: applications.py upload_application_media reads the full file into memory BEFORE checking the size cap. The 150 MB cap is enforced AFTER the bytes are already in RAM — a hostile client can still stream 150 MB into the worker process. Consider streaming with a max-bytes guard or relying on Starlette's max_upload_size at the ASGI layer for early short-circuit. Same pattern in submissions.py submission_upload.

resize_image_bytes fallback: both routers only attach resized_storage_path when `len(resized) < size_bytes`. For small / low-entropy originals (e.g. a 200 KB flat PNG) JPEG at 1600px can be larger than the source and the 'resized' copy is silently skipped. ClientView.jsx IMAGE_URL helper falls back to original, so behaviour is correct, but this makes the test_scale_p0 image test flaky unless a sufficiently large/noisy input is used.

Duplicate size-cap + resize code exists in submissions.py (L171-243) and applications.py (L171-203) — worth extracting a shared core helper (e.g. core._persist_upload_media) to stay DRY as more upload entry points are added.
Pagination helper _paginate_params/_paginated is correctly wired to keep the raw-list default. No breakage risk for existing FE calls that don't pass ?page.
Frontend SubmissionPage.jsx at 1462 lines exceeds the 700-line file-size guideline — split UploadSlot / TakeRow / AddTakeSlot / form sections into siblings (some are already defined inline).

success_rate.frontend: UI renders; progress-bar code paths verified via static review

seed_data_creation: Each test run creates throwaway projects (ScaleP0-* / ScaleP0x-*) and submissions/applications. No inline teardown for these; they accumulate in the DB across iterations. Recommend a periodic prune job or prefix-based cleanup fixture if this suite is run frequently.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend P0 scale work is green. Run `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v`. The progress-bar UI is hard to verify end-to-end in headless because localhost uploads finish instantly; to assert the stripe visually, throttle the network in Playwright (page.route with delayed response) or point the test at a remote URL. Public submit route is /submit/{slug} (not /audition/{slug}). The submission form has many required fields (age, height, location, availability, budget) that must be filled before the 'Save Details & Continue to Uploads' button unlocks the upload section — use admin API to pre-create a draft submission and jump to the upload section with the submitter token if you want to skip the form in future tests.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found in this iteration. Previously-reported AdminSubmissionEditIn.field_visibility bug from iteration_3 appears resolved (test_casting_review_live.py::test_public_link_custom_answers_modes now PASSES).
